inline_comments.message:
This is central to the schema creation, for simplicity and readability the collection utils class could be replaced with the following
ers -> ers.values().stream().map(EdgeRule::getLabel).collect(Collectors.toSet())
and some supporting comments.

To be honest, `ers -> ers.values().stream().map(EdgeRule::getLabel).collect(Collectors.toSet())` is less readable to me.

This is why I isolated this piece of code in CollectionUtils. This enables us to only mention what has to be done (EdgeRule::getLabel) instead of telling how it should be done (which is actually a best practice referred to as Local Reasoning).

What is the exact problem here? The passing of a function to a higher order function? Could you please elaborate a bit?

First statement it seems that is matter of opinion so no issue there.
Completely agree with the 2nd statement.
The need for an entire separate class for one method that is only used in 1 place seems a bit much.
If there are other use cases you are aware of for that method then its perfectly fine as is but some comments would be helpful otherwise can it be moved to the SchemaGenerator class with comments.

I have isolated the function in SchemaGenerator and set it as private. I've also added some javadoc (even though it's a private function). Initially I thought about putting in its own class, as collecting values is a pretty common need, but I agree that for this change, this may not be required. Let's wait for future cases to show up.

Thank you for your help.
